title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Correctness Defense
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
